question_structure.context: Cars A and B are $600\mathrm{m}$ apart and are traveling towards each other on a straight road at speeds of $10\mathrm{m/s}$ and $20\mathrm{m/s}$, respectively. A bird flies back and forth between the two cars at a speed of $20\mathrm{m/s}$. The bird starts from the front of Car A, flies towards Car B, and immediately turns around upon reaching the front of Car B to fly back towards Car A. This back-and-forth flight continues until the two cars meet.
question_structure.sub_question_1: The distance this little bird flies
question_structure.sub_question_2: The average speed of this small bird's flight

sub_question_1.step_1: Let the distance between the two cars be $d=600\mathrm{m}$, $v_{1}=10\mathrm{m/s}$, $v_{2}=20\mathrm{m/s}$, and the time until they meet be $t$. Then the distance between the two cars is: $d=v_{1}t + v_{2}t$
sub_question_1.step_2: The distance this small bird flies is: $s=v_{3}t$
sub_question_1.step_3: By simultaneously solving step_1 and step_2 and substituting the data, we obtain: $s=400\mathrm{m}$

sub_question_2.step_4: The displacement of the bird's flight is equal to the displacement of car A moving forward, that is: $x=v_{1}t$
sub_question_2.step_5: The average speed of the bird's flight is: $v=\frac{x}{t}$
sub_question_2.step_6: By simultaneously solving step_4 and step_5, and substituting the data, we can obtain: $v=10\mathrm{m/s}$

result_quantity.name: time until the two cars meet
The average speed of the bird
The average speed of the bird
